sanskrit:
अश्रद्दधानाः पुरुषा धर्मस्यास्य परन्तप।
अप्राप्य मां निवर्तन्ते मृत्युसंसारवर्त्मनि॥
transliteration: aśraddadhānāḥ puruṣā dharmasyāsya parantapa | aprāpya māṃ nivartante mṛtyu-saṃsāra-vartmani ||
literal_meaning: Those without faith, O scorcher of foes, not attaining Me, turn back on the path of the cycle of death.
contextual_meaning: Krishna warns Arjuna that intellectual or ritual engagement without inner conviction (śraddhā) collapses into renewed cycles of existential anxiety and self-defeat.
psychological_state: A client intellectually ‘knows’ the therapy tools yet secretly believes they won’t work; each relapse confirms the inner prophecy of unworthiness.
trigger_scenario: Signing up for a mindfulness app, using it for three days, then uninstalling and doom-scrolling at 2 a.m., convinced ‘nothing helps people like me.’
anchor_text: Doubt blocks the journey; trust the inner guide and keep walking.
